signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: RBNZ 2.5%. Despite a 125bp nominal carry disadvantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.5%), NZD outperformance is driven by the RBNZ's hawkish stance. COT LF net short -27,675 (long% 18.0%) represents a positioning extreme; with NZD as the G10 leader, a short-squeeze risk is elevated if data continues to support the RBNZ hike path.
signals.watch_level: Next RBNZ meeting on 2 Sep for hike confirmation.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Geopolitical Shock + Volatility Premium
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3525 under pressure following US strikes on Iran, breaking below the 1.3550 level. Options markets are pricing significant tail risk with GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 6.3%, creating a +4.7% vol risk premium. While OIS carry is neutral (-10bp favors GBP), the geopolitical escalation favors USD safe-haven flows over GBP's moderate negative CESI bias (-7 net misses).
signals.watch_level: Watch for sustained trading below 1.3500 to confirm bearish shift.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.11 remains bid by the +265bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.98%), but 25d RR at -1.39 signals the options market is paying a premium for JPY strength (downside protection). With the pair at 40-year highs, intervention risk is critical. JPY COT LF net short -104,231 (29.1% long) shows crowded carry positioning vulnerable to a sharp unwind if the BoJ shifts stance on 31 Jul.
signals.watch_level: Next BoJ meeting on 31 Jul for policy shift signals.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Volatility + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1468 testing upside as US PPI misses, despite a +145bp OIS carry disadvantage (SOFR 3.63% vs €STR 2.18%). A +2.0% VRP (IV 7.1% vs HV30 5.1%) suggests the market is hedging for higher volatility. However, a strong negative CESI (-14 misses) and net short COT positioning (lev_net -66,057) suggest the current advance lacks institutional backing and faces resistance near 1.1600.
signals.watch_level: Next ECB meeting on 23 Jul for guidance on hiking cycle.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4051 exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (z = -2.04σ), with the pair rising despite oil strength at $80.1. The BoC hold at 2.25% on 15 Jul reinforces the +150bp USD carry advantage. Retail sentiment is 62% short, diverging from the CAD COT net short of -87,846 (22.6% long), suggesting retail is unsuccessfully fading the institutional bearish CAD trend.
signals.watch_level: Watch for WTI break above $82 to test the correlation limit.
